response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with strong web, full-stack, and some systems-level experience, but the profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His only professional C++ work was a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015; the past decade has centered on Node.js, TypeScript, and DLang for web and data platforms. The role's core requirements — deep modern C++/STL, message-broker-based industrial systems, MQTT, and IEC 62443 secure development practices — are absent from his background, and there is no industrial automation or OT-security exposure to compensate.
strengths: Broad, sustained engineering career spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with recent staff-level roles at major tech companies
Real high-performance systems experience: low-latency services handling millions of requests per second using async I/O, fibers, and caching
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal and dated (about seven months, ending in 2015), against a role demanding hands-on C++/STL architecture
No exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL practices, MQTT, or OT protocols such as Modbus, OPC UA, or DNP 3.0
Career trajectory points firmly toward web and cloud product development, suggesting a poor motivational fit for embedded industrial C++ work

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 background
